days.summary: Frontend session UI, KeyVault naming, settings polish, and E2E coverage were updated alongside the backend cleanup.
frontendChangeBullets: Updated session screens so OS Agent and SDE Agent experiences present more consistent controls, labels, and state transitions.
Refined KeyVault wording across account setup, settings, and session launch surfaces so users see clearer key and provider terminology.
Improved settings polish with cleaner naming and more predictable navigation between key, session, and agent configuration areas.
Expanded end-to-end coverage around custom agent scenarios so session rendering regressions are easier to catch before release.
Tightened UI copy around agent architecture changes so product surfaces describe shared OS and SDE behavior more consistently.
backendChangeBullets: Unified more of the OS Agent and SDE Agent architecture so both runtime paths share consistent behavior and fewer duplicated code paths.
Cleaned up backend warnings across broad Rust areas to improve maintainability and reduce noise during development and testing.
Strengthened backend support for custom agent scenarios covered by new end-to-end checks.
Aligned key and session naming in backend-facing data paths with the updated product terminology.
Improved runtime stability by addressing cleanup work that affected agent sessions and backend service behavior.

days.summary: Agent-core work added multiple advanced runtime behaviors such as side queries, concurrent tools, microcompaction, away summaries, and dynamic tool descriptions, with E2E coverage. The UI gained session design tokens and playground previews, while project/session architecture, KeyVault source modeling, cross-platform Tauri flags, and Git integration were refined.
frontendChangeBullets: Added session UI design tokens to make chat surfaces, session cards, and agent states easier to style consistently.
Introduced playground previews for session UI so new chat layouts can be inspected before being rolled into core flows.
Improved visible behavior for long-running agent sessions with support for away summaries and richer progress communication.
Refined KeyVault source modeling in the UI so users can better understand where a session's model access comes from.
Polished project and session architecture surfaces around Git integration and cross-platform app behavior.
backendChangeBullets: Added side queries so agent runs can ask supporting questions without derailing the main turn.
Improved concurrent tool execution to let agent sessions handle multiple tool operations more efficiently.
Added microcompaction behavior to keep longer conversations usable while preserving important context.
Added dynamic tool descriptions so tools can present more relevant instructions based on runtime context.
Refined cross-platform Tauri flags, Git integration, and project/session architecture for a more consistent runtime base.

days.summary: The codebase tightened domain typing around MergeStatus, PriceTier, CliAgentType, KeySource, and provider identifiers while replacing hardcoded strings with constants. KeyVault moved toward backend-driven provider configuration, the model icon system was unified, and Rust validation/key-vault naming was cleaned up with supporting documentation and i18n pruning.
frontendChangeBullets: Unified model icon rendering into a single provider branding path for more consistent visuals across KeyVault and session screens.
Cleaned up localized provider and settings text so users see clearer labels for keys, sources, tiers, and agents.
Improved KeyVault UI behavior around backend-driven provider configuration instead of duplicated frontend assumptions.
Replaced hardcoded domain strings in frontend paths with typed constants for clearer and safer product behavior.
Tightened UI handling for MergeStatus, PriceTier, CliAgentType, KeySource, and provider identifiers across configuration flows.
backendChangeBullets: Added backend-driven provider configuration so KeyVault can rely on a more authoritative provider registry.
Tightened Rust and TypeScript domain typing for statuses, price tiers, key sources, CLI agents, and provider identifiers.
Cleaned up validation and key-vault naming so backend data structures better match product terminology.
Replaced hardcoded provider and status strings with constants to reduce runtime mismatch risks.
Pruned unused internationalization and provider naming paths to keep backend-facing configuration simpler.

days.summary: Backend configuration and definitions were split by concern, shared model defaults were made explicit, and the agent architecture continued removing silent fallbacks. Frontend work focused on status bar and layout cleanup, while usage history and session cache storage moved to Rust-backed SQLite and market API access was proxied through Tauri.
frontendChangeBullets: Improved status bar layout so session and app state indicators feel cleaner and less cluttered.
Polished Human Tools surfaces with layout cleanup and clearer embedded behavior.
Refined marketplace access from the UI so calls route through the app backend in a more consistent way.
Updated settings and layout surfaces around explicit model defaults and backend-backed persistence.
Cleaned up frontend paths that depended on silent fallback behavior so errors can be shown or handled more intentionally.
backendChangeBullets: Moved usage history persistence to Rust-backed SQLite for more reliable storage and querying.
Moved session cache storage to Rust-backed SQLite so cached session data survives more predictably.
Split backend configuration and definitions by concern to make runtime settings easier to reason about.
Made shared model defaults explicit instead of relying on implicit fallback behavior.
Proxied market API access through Tauri so marketplace integration follows a consistent backend path.

days.summary: Agent-core audit fixes continued while the toolbar and settings surfaces were reorganized around per-route and per-tab actions. Spotlight selectors were unified, session creator state was consolidated, dead session-loading paths were removed, and Human Tools/Integrations embedding received visual and lifecycle improvements.
frontendChangeBullets: Added per-route toolbar actions so pages can expose context-specific shortcuts instead of generic controls.
Added Add Repo and Add Folder shortcuts to the start page for faster project setup.
Reorganized settings surfaces around clearer per-tab actions and fewer session-specific paths.
Unified Spotlight selector behavior so users get a more consistent picker experience across workflows.
Polished embedded Human Tools and Integrations views with improved lifecycle and visual behavior.
backendChangeBullets: Continued agent-core audit fixes to reduce duplicated runtime paths and clarify agent behavior.
Consolidated session creator state so launch configuration is easier to manage across agent types.
Removed dead session-loading paths that no longer served the current session runtime.
Improved Integrations refresh behavior so backend policy changes are reflected after updates.
Tightened lifecycle handling for embedded Human Tools and integration surfaces.

days.summary: The agent runtime gained unified tool/result persistence, session memory, compaction robustness, JSON Schema Draft 7 tool parameters, and provider/key-vault registry cleanup. UI changes included model-chain compatibility filtering, event label standardization, chat grouping refinements, font token unification, and removal of obsolete atoms and aliases.
frontendChangeBullets: Added per-model enable and disable controls in model-chain configuration so users can tune routing more directly.
Standardized event labels in chat and simulator views for more readable session timelines.
Refined chat grouping behavior so related tool calls, results, and messages are easier to follow.
Unified font tokens across session UI surfaces for a more consistent visual rhythm.
Removed obsolete atoms and aliases from frontend state management to reduce confusing stale behavior.
backendChangeBullets: Added unified persistence for tool calls and tool results across agent sessions.
Improved session memory handling so longer conversations retain useful context more reliably.
Strengthened compaction robustness for extended agent runs with large histories.
Added JSON Schema Draft 7 handling for tool parameters to improve provider and tool compatibility.
Reduced proxy token inflation and fixed tool parameter handling in runtime paths.

days.summary: Agent-core parity work added unlimited loop handling, hooks, worktree, resume, streaming executor, project memory, dynamic tool descriptions, and warning/crash-safety behavior. Frontend and key-vault work added ghost filters, provider compatibility improvements, CLI streaming alignment, Kimi Code support, and removal of legacy analytics and dead atoms.
frontendChangeBullets: Added Kimi Code as a supported CLI agent option in user-facing selection flows.
Improved KeyVault ghost filtering so unavailable or irrelevant provider entries are less likely to confuse users.
Strengthened provider compatibility checks before users launch sessions with mismatched sources.
Aligned CLI streaming behavior in the UI so streamed output better matches agent runtime events.
Removed dead analytics and stale atoms to simplify frontend state and reduce unexpected UI updates.
backendChangeBullets: Added unlimited loop handling for agent sessions that need to continue across many tool turns.
Added runtime hooks, worktree awareness, resume behavior, and project memory to agent-core flows.
Improved streaming executor behavior for more reliable tool and message delivery.
Added dynamic tool descriptions and warning events to make runtime tool behavior safer and clearer.
Improved crash-safety behavior across agent sessions so failures are handled more predictably.

days.summary: Runtime hooks and memory controls were wired into the app, with Stop/SessionStop support, settings toggles, and time-based microcompaction. The session workflow stage surface was removed, channel routing was unified through Rust subtools, OpenCode CLI support landed, scratchpad parity was implemented, and interactive card UI was polished.
frontendChangeBullets: Added memory feature toggles in Indexing settings so users can control memory behavior from the app.
Removed the session workflow stage surface to simplify the visible session experience.
Added OpenCode as a supported CLI agent in setup and launch flows.
Polished interactive card UI for runtime hooks, scratchpad parity, and stream interruption states.
Improved chat feedback around Stop and SessionStop behavior so interruption feels more responsive.
backendChangeBullets: Wired Stop and SessionStop hooks into the agent runtime for cleaner interruption handling.
Added time-based microcompaction so long sessions can manage context pressure automatically.
Unified channel routing through Rust subtools for more consistent tool dispatch.
Implemented scratchpad parity across agent tools so shared notes behave consistently.
Improved stream interruption handling to reduce stuck or partially completed runtime states.

days.summary: Permission and autonomy handling was reworked into an agent-level model with persistent pattern rules, explicit account requirements, visible prompts, and E2E coverage. Chat rendering moved closer to direct SessionEvent consumption, cancellation and queue bugs were fixed, and read_file gained PDF, image, and notebook support.
frontendChangeBullets: Made approval prompts more visible so Ask-mode decisions reliably reach users.
Improved chat rendering by moving closer to direct SessionEvent consumption.
Refined autonomy controls so users can better understand persistent pattern-based permission rules.
Improved UI behavior around account requirements before agent actions are allowed.
Fixed high-priority chat queue and cancellation issues that could leave the interface in an unclear state.
backendChangeBullets: Reworked permissions into an agent-level autonomy model with persistent pattern rules.
Added explicit account requirement handling before runtime actions that need a connected account.
Added PDF support to read_file so agents can inspect documents through the standard file-reading path.
Added image and notebook support to read_file for richer multimodal and developer workflow context.
Fixed cancellation and queue bugs that affected runtime turn ordering and session responsiveness.

days.summary: Agent tools configuration was refined by hiding internal plumbing, adding sub-agent settings, renaming Search to Explore, wiring subagent tracking into the simulator, and adding turn-summary UI. Terminal lifecycle hardening, event-store canonical backfill, memory architecture simplification, and selected source persistence fixes rounded out the day.
frontendChangeBullets: Simplified tools configuration by hiding internal plumbing that users should not need to manage.
Added sub-agent settings so users can configure nested agent behavior more directly.
Renamed Search to Explore across product surfaces to better communicate discovery-oriented behavior.
Added turn-summary rendering in session UI so users can quickly understand what happened in a turn.
Improved selected-source persistence so session creation keeps the user's chosen source more reliably.
backendChangeBullets: Hardened terminal process lifecycle handling to reduce stuck or orphaned terminal states.
Added canonical event-store backfill so older session records can be interpreted more consistently.
Simplified memory architecture to reduce duplicated logic and improve runtime clarity.
Wired subagent tracking into the simulator for better test and replay visibility.
Refined agent tools configuration by separating internal runtime plumbing from user-configurable tools.

days.summary: The read_file tool absorbed image/PDF handling and the separate analyze_image path was removed. Session UI primitives were renamed from ChatBlock to EventBlock, a composable block recipe system and DevTools command picker were added, tool canonical names were unified with Rust, and the subagent spawning model was replaced with a unified agent tool plus await_output support.
frontendChangeBullets: Renamed chat rendering primitives from ChatBlock to EventBlock for clearer session UI architecture.
Added composable block recipes so tool output layouts can be assembled more consistently.
Added a DevTools command picker to help inspect and exercise session rendering behaviors.
Improved visual handling for image and PDF analysis by folding it into the standard file-reading flow.
Added UI support for background shell monitoring through await-output behavior.
backendChangeBullets: Folded image and PDF analysis into read_file and removed the separate analyze_image path.
Unified canonical tool names with Rust so frontend and backend refer to tools consistently.
Replaced the older subagent spawning model with a unified agent tool.
Added await_output support for monitoring background shell processes.
Added mode and background options to unified subagent launching for more flexible runtime execution.

days.summary: L3 memory was rebuilt across schema, write path, offline consolidation, salience, feedback, E2E coverage, and documentation. Event rendering was simultaneously reorganized so Rust owns payload extraction and frontend blocks consume structured extracted data through the SessionCore/SessionUI split.
frontendChangeBullets: Split SessionCore and SessionUI responsibilities so chat rendering code has cleaner boundaries.
Updated event blocks to consume structured extracted data instead of duplicating payload parsing in the UI.
Improved memory-related settings and indicators around automatic extraction and background consolidation.
Refined session rendering architecture so tool and event payloads appear more predictably.
Improved user-facing reliability for memory feedback by aligning UI flows with the rebuilt memory pipeline.
backendChangeBullets: Rebuilt long-term memory schema, write paths, salience handling, and feedback behavior.
Added offline consolidation for long-term memory so extracted knowledge can be organized after turns.
Wired automatic memory extraction into runtime configuration.
Moved event payload extraction into Rust so frontend blocks receive structured data.
Expanded end-to-end coverage around memory writes, consolidation, and session rendering behavior.

days.summary: Plan mode and interactive agent execution were heavily reworked: real Plan/Ask modes, Build approval flow, plan streaming, robust question matching, partial tool-call finalization, and provider ordered-content parsing were added. Await output, shell background events, planning indicators, and interactive tool routing received UI and runtime polish.
frontendChangeBullets: Overhauled Plan mode with visible Ask and Plan flows that better match user intent.
Added streaming plan cards so planning progress appears incrementally instead of only at completion.
Added Build approval handling so users can explicitly approve moving from planning into execution.
Improved planning indicators and interactive tool routing in chat.
Fixed partial tool-call finalization so interrupted streams are less likely to leave the UI blocked.
backendChangeBullets: Added real Plan and Ask runtime modes with clearer execution semantics.
Added automatic Build turns after approval so approved plans can continue into implementation flow.
Improved robust question matching for interactive agent execution.
Added provider ordered-content parsing for more faithful assistant response handling.
Improved await output and shell background events for long-running interactive tools.

days.summary: Session lifecycle behavior was aligned around queue, stop, regenerate, ask-user, and per-session file history semantics. Subagent rendering and persistence were simplified and race-hardened, session identity resolution removed silent fallbacks, and simulator replay/autoplay/follow behavior received fixes.
frontendChangeBullets: Aligned session lifecycle UI with queue, stop, regenerate, and ask-user flows.
Added per-session file history so users can revisit files associated with a specific conversation.
Added regeneration confirmation to reduce accidental reruns of agent work.
Improved silent-queue cancellation UX so users get clearer feedback when queued work is stopped.
Made nested subagent rendering more reliable with race-safe cache loading.
backendChangeBullets: Aligned runtime lifecycle behavior around queue, stop, regenerate, and ask-user semantics.
Improved per-session file history handling in backend session state.
Simplified subagent persistence and rendering data paths.
Hardened subagent cache loading against races during replay and session switching.
Removed silent fallbacks from session identity resolution so incorrect identity states surface earlier.

days.summary: Subagent scratchpad sharing and prompt injection were tested and repaired, background job reminders and async turn summaries were added, and stream retry/backoff behavior was introduced. The day also focused on crash/restart cancellation edge cases, memory pipeline repair, housekeeping cleanup, and richer simulator subagent grids/replay layouts.
frontendChangeBullets: Added richer simulator subagent grids so nested agent runs are easier to inspect.
Improved replay layouts for subagent and background job activity.
Added visible async turn summaries to help users understand what happened after long-running work.
Improved cancellation recovery UX when interrupted streams produce partial output.
Refined reminders and background job surfaces for clearer follow-up on pending work.
backendChangeBullets: Restored scratchpad sharing with subagents and added regression coverage.
Added stream retry budgets and backoff behavior for more resilient provider streaming.
Improved crash and restart cancellation edge cases with snapshot-based output detection.
Repaired memory reflection, extraction, and consolidation paths.
Added background job reminders and async turn summaries to support longer-running agent workflows.

days.summary: Rules, skills, and todo behavior were aligned with the current agent workflow model: policies were renamed to rules, slash commands began injecting SKILL. md content, built-in create-agent/rule/skill flows were refreshed, blocked task DAG metadata was propagated, and ask-user/nag reminder scenarios were pinned by E2E tests. Documentation and phase-numbering cleanup accompanied the runtime changes.
frontendChangeBullets: Renamed policies to rules across user-facing agent management surfaces.
Refreshed create-rule and create-skill flows so users get clearer built-in setup experiences.
Added built-in create-agent support for faster agent creation from guided flows.
Improved todo UI behavior around blocked-task relationships and reminder prompts.
Updated visible terminology for rules, skills, and agent behavior to align with the agent workflow model.
backendChangeBullets: Aligned rules, skills, and todo behavior more closely with the current agent workflow model.
Injected skill content into conversations when slash commands run.
Propagated blocked task DAG metadata so todo dependencies can be represented in runtime state.
Pinned ask-user and nag reminder scenarios with end-to-end tests.
Cleaned up phase numbering and runtime documentation around the updated rules and skills behavior.

days.summary: OS Agent personal workspace configuration, routines, Launchpad quick actions, and simulator subagent bug fixes were also added.
frontendChangeBullets: Added OS Agent personal workspace configuration surfaces with clearer layered settings behavior.
Added Launchpad quick actions for faster access to common OS Agent workflows.
Improved simulator reliability for subagent scenarios that previously displayed inconsistent state.
Refined MCP notifications so tool activity is easier to understand in the app.
Improved visible handling of MCP image results through OpenAI-compatible vision support.
backendChangeBullets: Rebuilt MCP support on a newer bridge with structured tool results.
Improved MCP notification delivery so tool progress and results are easier to track.
Added OpenAI-compatible vision handling for MCP image results.
Unified OS Agent personal workspace configuration with layered settings merge behavior.
Improved gateway smart routing and fixed simulator subagent reliability issues.

days.summary: Human Tools and browser surfaces advanced significantly: browser inspector controls, webview layering, selected DOM-to-chat pills, page screenshots, preview routing, binary/video previews, and launchpad tabs were added or polished. MCP UI gained progress and wizard suggestions, while IPC payload casing and import aliases were fixed.
frontendChangeBullets: Added browser inspector controls for more direct interaction with embedded browser surfaces.
Added selected-DOM chat pills so users can send chosen page elements into a conversation.
Added page screenshot attachments for browser-driven agent workflows.
Improved webview layering so browser previews sit correctly behind React overlays.
Introduced the Idea Area page with trending, shared, and personal idea tabs.
backendChangeBullets: Improved browser and Human Tools support for page screenshots and selected DOM context.
Added preview routing support for browser and binary preview surfaces.
Added binary and video preview handling to broaden file and browser inspection workflows.
Added MCP progress plumbing so the UI can show richer server activity.
Fixed IPC payload casing and import aliases that affected integration reliability.

days.summary: WorkStation and MCP management received a large redesign with per-server controls, grouped tools, bulk toggles, uptime tracking, faster connect-all behavior, and E2E MCP coverage. Navigation moved toward URL-driven MainApp routes and shared spotlight selector shells, while SessionWorkspace planning documentation was expanded.
frontendChangeBullets: Redesigned MCP server management with per-server controls for clearer individual server operation.
Added grouped MCP tools and bulk toggles so users can manage large tool sets faster.
Added uptime tracking to MCP server management for clearer operational status.
Improved WorkStation navigation and shared Spotlight palette behavior.
Moved MainApp navigation toward URL-driven routes with automatic breadcrumbs.
backendChangeBullets: Improved MCP connect-all behavior so server startup is faster and more reliable.
Expanded end-to-end MCP coverage to validate server management behavior.
Added backend support for per-server MCP controls, grouped tools, and bulk state changes.
Improved route state support for URL-driven MainApp navigation.
Expanded SessionWorkspace planning documentation to guide the central workspace runtime concept.

days.summary: SessionWorkspace became a central runtime concept, with persistence, mutator commands, hot refresh for file tools, and gateway-driven mutation support. Integrations custom-tool residue was removed, built-in tools hooks were unified, simulator/chat UX was polished, and WorkStation/browser/sidebar layout cleanup continued.
frontendChangeBullets: Polished WorkStation, browser, sidebar, simulator, and chat layouts around the new SessionWorkspace concept.
Allowed workspace changes to be applied from chat through a guided gateway flow.
Removed custom-tool UI residue to simplify Integrations and built-in tool management.
Improved simulator and chat UX around workspace-aware file tools.
Cleaned up sidebar and browser layouts so workspace context feels more consistent.
backendChangeBullets: Added persistent SessionWorkspace support as a central runtime concept.
Added mutator commands for changing SessionWorkspace state.
Added hot refresh for file tools when workspace configuration changes.
Added gateway-driven mutation support so chat-initiated workspace changes reach runtime state.
Unified built-in tool hooks and removed leftover custom-tool paths.

days.summary: The project retired AgentConfig and legacy LearningConfig scaffolding while tightening plan approval, tool_call_id propagation, reflection write paths, L3 consolidation rules, and websocket event payload casing. Work also added active-observation L3 writes, plan approval persistence, Build identity handling, and extensive E2E pins around memory and planning regressions.
frontendChangeBullets: Persisted plan approvals across restarts so approved Build flows remain understandable after reopening the app.
Preserved Build-session identity in user-facing planning flows.
Improved planning regression coverage to protect visible Plan and Build behavior.
Retired obsolete agent configuration scaffolding that no longer mapped to current settings surfaces.
Improved memory-related user outcomes through active-observation long-term memory writes.
backendChangeBullets: Retired AgentConfig and old LearningConfig scaffolding from runtime paths.
Tightened plan approval persistence so approval state survives restarts.
Improved tool_call_id propagation for more reliable tool dispatch and result matching.
Added active-observation writes to long-term memory.
Tightened reflection write paths, consolidation rules, and websocket event payload casing.

days.summary: Gateway routing was retired or simplified into OS session/channel paths, while channel messaging gained batching, group isolation, background protection, idle reset, and workspace-folder wiring. Agent API debug/test endpoints were split into focused modules, housekeeping retention cleanup was implemented, and tribal-code naming was scrubbed.
frontendChangeBullets: Improved chat behavior for channel messages with batching, group isolation, and clearer background protection.
Refined SessionWorkspace folder wiring for IDE integration and workspace-aware UI behavior.
Simplified visible gateway-related flows as channel messages moved directly into OS sessions.
Improved session deletion behavior so related data cleanup is safer and less surprising.
Cleaned up user-facing naming that had drifted from the current product language.
backendChangeBullets: Simplified gateway routing so channel messages flow directly into OS session and channel paths.
Added channel batching, group isolation, idle reset, and background protection.
Split Agent API debug and test endpoints into focused modules.
Added full-sweep housekeeping cleanup with safer session deletion cascades.
Improved SessionWorkspace folder wiring for IDE integration.

days.summary: Agent runtime initialization was simplified by removing relay structs, consolidating session lookup, deriving singletons from app handles, and renaming workspace fields to working_dir/project_root. Tool availability became capability-derived, gateway references were purged, Wingman gained a full chat panel, and tracked Swift build artifacts were removed.
frontendChangeBullets: Added a full Wingman chat view with a floating panel and bar toggle.
Improved workspace terminology in UI-facing fields by clarifying working directory and project root concepts.
Simplified session identity behavior so users are less likely to see mismatched session context.
Improved tool availability presentation by deriving capabilities from runtime state.
Removed tracked Swift build artifacts that could clutter project views and developer workflows.
backendChangeBullets: Simplified agent runtime initialization by removing relay structs and consolidating session lookup.
Derived runtime singletons from app handles for cleaner initialization.
Renamed workspace fields to working_dir and project_root for clearer runtime identity.
Derived tool availability from capabilities instead of layered deny lists.
Purged gateway references from runtime paths that now use direct session and channel handling.

days.summary: Agent-core underwent a broad modularization and dead-surface cleanup, splitting memory, desktop, wingman, AgentTool, session runtime, and post-turn work into smaller modules. Desktop/AX safety advanced with Objective-C exception barriers and review fixes, missing Tauri commands were registered, and empty resume messages were prevented.
frontendChangeBullets: Improved Wingman-related UI reliability by supporting the newly split runtime modules.
Restored affected app features by registering missing Tauri command handlers.
Prevented empty resume messages from creating confusing session UI states.
Continued cleanup of dead product surfaces so users see fewer stale controls.
Improved desktop automation safety outcomes that affect visible Human Tools behavior.
backendChangeBullets: Split large memory, desktop, Wingman, AgentTool, session runtime, and post-turn modules into focused pieces.
Added Objective-C exception barriers for safer desktop accessibility automation calls.
Registered missing Tauri command handlers that affected app feature availability.
Prevented empty resume messages from entering runtime session flows.
Continued broad dead-surface cleanup across agent-core modules.

days.summary: Agent-core session, intelligence, foundation, and tools audits continued, removing legacy memory search paths and retiring snapshot flags. Desktop worker isolation progressed through IPC framing hardening, AX-FFI surface retirement, worker-routed capture/action dispatch, and scroll boundary detection, while My Station UI was simplified.
frontendChangeBullets: Simplified the My Station interface so users encounter a cleaner workspace surface.
Improved desktop automation behavior that affects capture, action dispatch, and scrolling experiences.
Reduced exposure of outdated memory search behavior in user-facing agent flows.
Improved reliability of desktop capture actions by routing them through isolated worker paths.
Refined scroll boundary behavior so desktop automation feels less likely to overshoot.
backendChangeBullets: Continued agent-core session, intelligence, foundation, and tools audits.
Removed old memory search paths and retired snapshot flags from runtime behavior.
Advanced desktop worker isolation with stronger IPC framing.
Retired AX-FFI surfaces in favor of worker-routed capture and action dispatch.
Added scroll boundary detection for safer desktop automation.

days.summary: A file-size and concern-splitting sweep broke many oversized agent-core modules into focused submodules, including execute_turn, persistence messages, channel handler, desktop capture, provider streaming, MCP manager/client, tool implementations, and memory learning files. UI work included ProjectManager and routines fixes plus a floating task creator.
frontendChangeBullets: Improved ProjectManager behavior with targeted UI fixes.
Improved routines behavior so recurring workflow surfaces behave more reliably.
Added a floating task creator for faster task capture from product workflows.
Reduced user-facing instability by splitting oversized runtime modules behind session and tool surfaces.
Improved frontend maintainability around ProjectManager and task workflow areas.
backendChangeBullets: Split oversized execute_turn, persistence message, and channel handler modules into focused submodules.
Split desktop capture and provider streaming code into smaller runtime units.
Split MCP manager and client code for clearer server integration responsibilities.
Split tool implementation and memory learning files into more maintainable pieces.
Reduced large-file complexity across agent infrastructure for more reliable iteration.

days.summary: Agent-core cleanup continued with architecture docs updated, test modules extracted, clippy warnings cleared, helper modules split, magic strings retired, and tool/category single sources of truth improved. Control Tower and browser UI received tab bar, favicon, git sidebar, sticky header, and simulator follow-app fixes.
frontendChangeBullets: Added the Control Tower Git section to the sidebar for easier source-control access.
Improved browser tab bar behavior and favicon rendering.
Improved sticky headers so long pages maintain better context while scrolling.
Fixed simulator follow-app behavior so replay and live app views stay aligned more reliably.
Improved browser UI polish around tab, icon, and header presentation.
backendChangeBullets: Updated agent-core architecture documentation to reflect current runtime structure.
Extracted test modules to make agent-core testing easier to maintain.
Cleared agent-core warnings for cleaner Rust validation.
Split helper modules to reduce large utility files and improve code ownership.
Retired magic strings in tool category handling and strengthened single sources of truth.

days.summary: A major robustness and cleanup sweep surfaced formerly swallowed errors, preserved corrupt config/rules files, restored delete_file as a first-class tool, tightened visibility, removed dead commands and test helpers, and continued project workspace cleanup. Agent-core and project-management paths saw the largest churn, with documentation tracking the cleanup backlog.
frontendChangeBullets: Improved user-visible error handling by surfacing runtime and history-loading failures that were previously hidden.
Preserved corrupt automation rules files so users have a better chance to recover broken configuration.
Restored delete-file as a first-class agent tool in product workflows.
Tightened visibility around project workspace cleanup so broken states are less likely to be silently ignored.
Reduced dead command surfaces that could otherwise appear available but not work correctly.
backendChangeBullets: Surfaced formerly swallowed errors across runtime and history-loading paths.
Preserved corrupt config and rules files instead of overwriting or dropping them.
Restored delete_file as a first-class backend agent tool.
Removed dead commands and unused test helpers from backend paths.
Continued project workspace cleanup with stricter error visibility and backlog tracking.

days.summary: The busiest day of the month focused on runtime identity and agent-org infrastructure: project_path/working_dir/code_repo_path projections were unified, session identity resolver matrices were closed, key_source persisted end-to-end, typed inter-agent messaging was introduced, and worker sub-sessions began resolving org context through parent sessions. A broad silent-fallback sweep surfaced many previously ignored filesystem, JSON, DB, lock, telemetry, shell, and HTTP errors.
frontendChangeBullets: Improved user-facing consistency around project path, working directory, and code repository identity.
Improved session behavior for worker sub-sessions by resolving organization context through parent sessions.
Strengthened visible key-source behavior so Rust agent sessions retain the correct source across flows.
Improved collaboration reliability through typed inter-agent messaging.
Surfaced more filesystem, JSON, database, telemetry, shell, and HTTP errors so users are not left with silent failures.
backendChangeBullets: Unified project_path, working_dir, and code_repo_path projections across runtime identity handling.
Closed session identity resolver matrices for more reliable session and workspace resolution.
Persisted key_source end-to-end for Rust agent sessions.
Introduced typed inter-agent messaging for clearer agent-org communication.
Surfaced previously hidden filesystem, JSON, database, lock, telemetry, shell, and HTTP errors.

days.summary: Group chat and inbox UX became the main focus, adding reply routing, deduped broadcasts, hover action trays, edit-members flows, lifecycle messages, chat bubbles, task cards, and View Diff buttons. Agent-org work added inbox auto-resume and slimmer runtime messages, while session creator icons, simulator follow mode, typed constants, and project/group chat backend paths were refined.
frontendChangeBullets: Added group chat reply routing so responses land in the correct collaboration thread.
Added deduplicated broadcasts to reduce repeated group chat updates.
Improved group chat UX with hover action trays, edit-members flows, lifecycle messages, chat bubbles, and task cards.
Added View Diff buttons on completed group-chat session cards.
Improved session creator icons, simulator follow mode, and inbox follow-up behavior.
backendChangeBullets: Added inbox auto-resume behavior for agent organization collaboration.
Slimmed agent-org messages for cleaner runtime communication.
Refined project and group chat backend paths that support replies, broadcasts, and lifecycle messages.
Added typed constants around group chat and session behavior to reduce string drift.
Improved backend support for completed session cards and diff viewing in collaboration flows.
